observations.recommendation: Due to Officers and Employees and Interest Payable the soonest possible time to avoid any inconveniences in the future.

We recommend to the management the following:
3.1 Local Chief Executive

a. Abides to Section 5.1 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.
b. Sends Demand Letters to personnel concerned with unliquidated Cash Advance.
c. Supervises and monitors the granting of cash advances to personnel concerned unless previous cash advances have been liquidated.
3.2 Municipal Accountant
a. Submits the list of personnel with unliquidated cash advances.
b. Advises the LCE on the status of the cash advances and liquidations of the personnel concerned with unliquidated cash advances.
3.3 Personnel concerned.
a. Liquidate their respective cash advances.
We recommend to the Accounting management to conduct negotiation with Philippine National Bank (PNB) and the Philippine Postal Saving Bank (PPSB).

a) Adhere to Section 57 of Republic Act (RA) No. 464, otherwise known
as the Real Tax Code and Section 470 (d) (2) of the Republic Act (RA)
No. 7160, otherwise known as the Local Government Code of 1991,
b) Exert extra effort collect the Real Property Tax,
c) Conduct tax campaign/drive to the real property tax payers and
d) Provide tax incentives through the Sangguniang Bayan to legislate for
that purpose.

In line with the discussion above, we recommended to the LCE to instruct
MPDC to submit the inventory of local roads duly reconciled with the road
map as recorded in the Road Networks Account and the Municipal
Accountant to maintain the subsidiary ledgers on the local roads.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the
General Services Office (GSO) to submit the RPCPPE and maintain the
Property Acknowledgment Receipt, pursuant to Section 122 of PD 1445
and Section 21 of the Government Accounting Manual, For National
Government Agencies, Volume I.

a) Municipal Budget Officer to update the RAOMO and RAOCO in
accordance with Section 119 of PD 1445 and
b) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer reconcile their
records.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the
personnel concerned to settle their respective Notice of Suspension,
pursuant to the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-006 dated
September 15, 2009.

Enforce settlement of audit disallowance in accordance with Section 7.1.1 of the Public and Regulations on Settlement of Accounts. Ensure also that government accounting rules and regulations are strictly complied with before processing claims/payments to avoid incurrence of disallowances.

We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the following officials concerned:
5.1 Municipal Mayor
5.1.1 Abide to Section 4 of the Republic Act (RA) No. 5447, otherwise known as An Act creating the Special Education Fund.
5.2 Municipal Treasurer
5.2.1 Rent Property Tax Receivable totaling to ₱12,198,108.00 of the previous administration
5.2.2 Abide to Section 57 of the Presidential Decree (PD) No. 464, otherwise known as the Real Property Tax Code
5.2.3 Conduct tax campaign especially to those real property owners.
5.2.4 Exert extra effort to collect the real property taxes.
5.2.5 Recommend to the Sangunian Bayan for the tax legislation on tax incentives for those real property owners who would pay their respective taxes early.
5.3 Municipal Assessor
5.3.1 Adhere to Section 122 of the Presidential Decree (PD) No.1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.
5.3.2 Submit the listing of the Real Property Tax owners with computation.
5.4 Municipal Accountant
5.4.1 Due from Officers and Employees Account
5.4.1.1 Adhere to Section 116 of the Presidential Decree (PD) No.1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.
5.4.2 Special Education Tax Receivable.
5.4.2.1 Special Education Tax Receivable Account Abide to Section 4 of the Republic Act (RA) No.5447 otherwise known as An Act creating a Special Education Fund.
5.4.2.2 To transfer ₱1,734,317.85 from the General Fund to the Special Education Fund for the said collectibles under the Special Education Tax Receivable Account.

We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal Accountant to remit to the Government Service and Insurance System (GSIS) the Due to GSIS totaling to ₱2,046,518.18 and to the Home Development Mutual Fund (HDMF) of ₱366,543.62 the Due to PAGIBIG in accordance with Section 3.4 Rule III of the Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRRs) of the Republic Act (RA) No. 8291, otherwise known as the GSIS Act of 1997 and Section 3 of the Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRRs) of the Republic Act (RA) No. 9679 or the Home Development Mutual Fund (HDMF) of 2009, otherwise known as the PAG IBIG (Pagtutulungan sa Kinabukasan: Ikaw, Bangko, Industriya at Gobyerno) Fund.

To abide Section 3.0 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020. 7.2 To create the inventory committee. 7.3 To conduct inventory of Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE).

In relation to this, we recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the following personnel to comply:
8.1 Local Chief Executive
8.1.1 To apply the guidelines and procedures on inventory taking in accordance with Section 3.0 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
8.1.2 To create the Inventory Committee composing of the Accounting, Property Divisions/Units and Internal Audit Service/Unit for the purpose of cleansing the PPE Account in accordance with Section 5.2 of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
8.2 Inventory Committee
8.2.1 To prepare the Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) in accordance with Section 5.9 of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
8.2.2 To conduct the Physical Inventory taking pursuant to Section 5.3 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
8.2.3 To prepare the Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) in pursuant of Section 6.2.13 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
8.3 Property Unit
8.3.1 To ensure that the recording are updated pursuant to Section 6.3.1(a) of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
8.3.2 To prepare Property Cards for items found at station, in accordance with Section 6.3.1(c) of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
8.3.3 To prepare list of non existing/missing PPEs in accordance with Section 6.3.1(d) of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
8.3.4 To prepare Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) in accordance with Section 6.3.1(h) of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
8.3.5 To reconcile with the Accounting Unit in accordance with Section 6.3.1(f) of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
8.4 Accounting Unit
8.4.1 To take necessary accounting entries to recognize PPEs found at station and prepare corresponding Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards based on the List of PPEs found at Station in accordance with Section 6.3.2(a) of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
8.4.2 To take up necessary accounting entries to recognize loss of PPE and to set up corresponding receivables from concerned accountable officer pursuant to Section 6.3.2(b) of the COA Circular No. 2020-006.
8.4.3 To reconcile with the Property Unit, pursuant to Section 6.3.2(c) of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
8.4.4 To take up necessary accounting entries to write-off/drop from the books of accounts the remaining remaining balance as of December 31, 2021, pursuant to Section 6.3.2(e) of COA Circular No. 2020-006.
8.4.5 To ensure the total balance of PPELCs/SLs totally with the balances of controlling PPE accounts in the General Ledgers, pursuant to Section 6.3.2(f) of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
8.5 Internal Audit Service/Unit
8.5.1 To witness the conduct of the inventory in accordance with Section 5.5 of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

We recommend the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the above personnel mentioned below the following:
9.1 Supply Officer
9.1.1 Abide to Section D.7 of the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Water Vehicle.
9.1.2 Prepare the Monthly Fuel Consumption (MFC).
9.1.3 Submit to the Auditor the Driver’s Trip Tickets (DTT) and Monthly Fuel Consumption.
9.2 Drivers
9.2.1 To adhere to Section D.6 of the above Manual.
9.2.2 To prepare the Monthly Report of Official Travels (MROT) where he summarizes in chronological order his trip for the month.
9.2.3 To submit the above report to the Chief Accountant.
9.3 Chief Accountant
9.3.1 To submit the Monthly Report of Official Travels of the Drivers to the Auditor.

In view of this, we recommend the management of the Municipal Government of Matanog, Maguindanao to adopt the uniform property identification system, pursuant to Sections 5.6 and 5.7 of the Commission on Audit (COA)

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDC) the following:
11.1 To prepare and submit the list of all on-going projects relative to its programs/projects/activities to be implemented at the start of the current year.
11.2 Prepare and submit monthly report on the status of the said projects/programs.

We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the personnel concerned to settle the Notice of Suspension, pursuant to Section 9.4 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommend that the Municipal Government of Matanog, Maguindanao should settle the Due to Officers and Interest Payable Account at the soonest possible time to avoid any inconveniences in the future.

observations.managementAction: 1. The audit management suspended the payment of Salary Public and Regulations on Settlement of Cash Accounts. Ensure that Settlement of the Cash Advance.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the following officials to the case concerned: 5.1 Municipal Mayor 5.1.1 Abide to Section 4 of the Republic Act (RA) No. 5447, otherwise known as An Act creating Special Education Fund. 5.2 Municipal Treasurer 5.2.1 Rent Property Tax to Receivable Account totaling ₱12,198,108.00. 5.2.2 Abide to Section 57 of the Presidential Decree (PD) No. 464. 5.2.3 Conduct tax campaign especially to those real property owners. 5.2.4 Exert extra effort to collect the real property taxes. 5.2.5 Recommend to the Sangguniang Bayan for tax legislation on tax incentives for those real property owners who would pay their respective taxes early. 5.3 Municipal Assessor 5.3.1 Adhere to Section 122 of the Presidential Decree (PD) No.1445. 5.3.2 Submit the listing of the Real Property Tax owners with computation. 5.4 Municipal Accountant 5.4.1 Due from Officers and Employees Account 5.4.1.1 Adhere to Section 116 of the Presidential Decree (PD) No.1445. 5.4.1.2 Reclassify the Due from Offices and Employees Account to Receivables - Disallowances Account of ₱12,198,108.00. 5.4.2 Special Education Tax Receivable. 5.4.2.1 Special Education Tax Receivable Account Abide to Section 4 of the Republic Act (RA) No.5447 otherwise known as An Act creating a Special Education Fund. 5.4.2.2 To transfer ₱1,734,317.85 from the General Fund to the Special Education Fund for the said collectibles under the Special Education Tax Receivable Account.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal Accountant to remit to the Government Service and Insurance System (GSIS) totaling to ₱2,046,518.18 and to the Home Development Fund (HDMF) of ₱366,543.62 in accordance with Section 3.4 Rule III and Section 3 of the IRRs of RA 8291 and RA 9679.
observations.managementAction: Management remitted the amounts and signed a Memorandum of Agreement with the GSIS.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the Municipal Treasurer to: Abide to Section 57 of PD No. 464; Conduct campaign/drive in the collection of the Real Property Tax (RPT) as well as other taxes; Exert effort in the collection of the Real Property Taxes.
observations.managementAction: Management allocated some of the taxes. Conducted some collection activities.
observations.reason: Some taxpayers were reluctant to pay.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Local Chief Executive to: 9.1 Create a committee to conduct an immediate physical count of all its property, plant and equipment. 9.2 Require each Department Head to conduct every year a physical inventory count of all properties under their office and to submit inventory report to the inventory committee for the random validation of the existence of the property; 9.3 Require both the Accountant and General Services Officer to maintain and update records and ledgers of all properties owned by the Municipality; 9.4 Require the General Services Officer to attend seminar related to property management.
observations.managementAction: Implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the personnel concerned to settle their respective Notice of Suspension, in accordance with Section 9.4 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
observations.managementAction: The personnel concerned settled some notices and the management directed settlement.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to the Municipal Accountant to ensure timely preparation and submission of the Trial Balances, Journal Entry Vouchers and the Statement of Appropriation, Allotments and Obligations within the prescribed period for proper monitoring and timely decision making, pursuant to Sections 70 and 72 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: Implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer: 12.1 Strict adherence to Item No. 2.1 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2005-001 dated February 28, 2005. 12.2 Maintain the required registries of funds/appropriations/allotments and obligations and utilization in order to avoid misappropriation of funds and for internal control purposes and to facilitate the realization of the programs/projects/activities embodied in the Approved Annual Budget.
observations.managementAction: Implemented.

observations.recommendation:
60. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive to:
60.1 Create a committee to conduct an immediate physical count of all its
property, plant and equipment. They are required to submit the
RPCPPE on or before January 31 of each year, copy furnished the
COA Auditor concerned, pursuant to Section 156 of the Commission
on Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992 and
Section 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting
System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units.
60.2 Require each Department Head to conduct every year a physical
inventory count of all properties under their office and to submit
inventory report to the inventory committee for the random
validation of the existence of the property;
60.3 Require both the Accountant and General Services Officer to
maintain and update records and ledgers all properties owned by the
Municipality; and
60.4 Require the General Services Officer to attend seminar related to
property management.

observations.recommendation: 5. We recommend that the LGU shall submit the disbursement orders for the period September to December 2013 for post audit and examination. Continued disregard will be dealt with accordingly pursuant to the above stated provision of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: 5. Implemented.

observations.recommendation: 8. The LGU shall submit the disbursement vouchers for the period September to December, 2013 for post audit and examination. Continued disregard will be dealt with accordingly pursuant to the above stated provision of PD 1445.

9. We recommend that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the Disbursing Officer and Officers and Employees to settle their respective cash advances, pursuant to Section 89 of Presidential Decree No.1445, otherwise known as the State Audit Code of the Philippines.
observations.managementAction: 5. Some Cash Advances were those of the previous administration.
observations.reason: 5. The great bulk of the Cash Advances were those of the previous administration.

observations.recommendation: 13. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the personnel concerned to settle their respective Notice of Suspension within the 90 days period upon receipt, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
observations.managementAction: 13. Implemented.

observations.recommendation: 14. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Accountable Officer and the Municipal Accountant to submit the Cashbooks on the Cash in Treasury and Cash Advances; together with the Bank Statements and Bank Reconciliation Statements together with the Bank Statements and Subsidiary Ledgers, pursuant to Section 100 of Presidential Decree No.1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.
observations.managementAction: 14. Implemented.

observations.recommendation: 18. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the personnel concerned to submit the required reports and paid disbursement vouchers together with its supporting documents within the prescribed period, pursuant to Sections 100 and 122 of Presidential Decree No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.
observations.managementAction: 19. Implemented.

observations.recommendation: 19. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the complete books of accounts, pursuant to Section 111 of the Presidential Decree No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.
observations.managementAction: 19. Implemented.

observations.recommendation: 20. We commend the Management of Matanog, Matuindanao for its effort in the implementation of the Gender and Development (GAD).
observations.managementAction: 20. Implemented.
